timeline.event: Born in Hyde Park, New York.
His father, James Roosevelt I, died.
Graduated from Harvard University in 3 years.
Enrolled in Columbia Law School.
Married Eleanor Roosevelt.
Passed the New York bar exam and dropped out of Columbia Law School.
Elected to the New York State Senate.
Inaugurated as New York State Senator.
Re-elected to the New York State Senate.
Appointed Assistant Secretary of the Navy under Woodrow Wilson.
Eleanor Roosevelt discovered his affair with Lucy Mercer.
Ran as James M. Cox's vice presidential running mate but was defeated.
His lower body became paralyzed due to illness and was diagnosed with polio.
Established a rehabilitation center in Warm Springs, Georgia.
Elected Governor of New York.
The Wall Street Crash occurred and the Great Depression began.
Re-elected as Governor of New York.
Won the US presidential election against Herbert Hoover.
Survived an assassination attempt by Giuseppe Zangara.
Inaugurated as the 32nd President of the United States.
Declared a four-day 'bank holiday'.
The Emergency Banking Act was passed.
Signed the Cullen-Harrison Act, ending Prohibition.
Implemented the National Industrial Recovery Act (NIRA).
Reformed financial regulations through the Glass-Steagall Act.
Established the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
Established the Federal Communications Commission (FCC).
The US Supreme Court declared the National Industrial Recovery Act unconstitutional.
The Social Security Act was passed.
Established the Works Progress Administration (WPA).
Re-elected in a landslide victory in the US presidential election.
Failed to pass the Judicial Procedures Reform Bill.
The Housing Act was passed.
Founded the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis.
The Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) was passed.
Passed the Reorganization Act and established the Executive Office of the President.
World War II broke out with Germany's invasion of Poland.
Won the US presidential election, becoming the only president to serve more than three terms.
Delivered the 'Four Freedoms' speech.
Obtained congressional approval for the Lend-Lease Act.
Received a declaration of war from Japan following the attack on Pearl Harbor.
Germany and Italy declared war on the United States.
Died.

keyAchievements.title: Longest-serving US President
keyAchievements.description: He is the only president in US history to serve the longest and complete more than three terms.

keyAchievements.title: Implementation of the New Deal
keyAchievements.description: Implemented the New Deal policies to overcome the Great Depression, providing relief to the unemployed and farmers, and seeking economic recovery.

keyAchievements.title: Political realignment and pioneering modern liberalism
keyAchievements.description: Built the 'New Deal Coalition', reorganizing US politics into the Fifth Party System and pioneering modern liberalism.

keyAchievements.title: Financial and labor reforms
keyAchievements.description: Introduced major regulatory reforms in finance, communications, and labor, such as establishing the SEC and the FDIC, and enacting the National Labor Relations Act.

keyAchievements.title: Repeal of Prohibition
keyAchievements.description: Signed the Cullen-Harrison Act to end the Prohibition era.

keyAchievements.title: Leadership during World War II
keyAchievements.description: Worked closely with other national leaders to lead the Allied Powers against the Axis Powers and oversaw the economic mobilization of the United States.

keyAchievements.title: Initiation of nuclear weapon development
keyAchievements.description: Initiated the development of the world's first nuclear weapons.

keyAchievements.title: Laying the foundation for the United Nations
keyAchievements.description: Cooperated with other Allied leaders to lay the foundation for the United Nations and other postwar institutions.

keyAchievements.title: Promotion of the Good Neighbor Policy
keyAchievements.description: Promoted the Good Neighbor Policy, renouncing the right to unilaterally intervene in the affairs of Latin American countries.

keyAchievements.title: Establishment of the Social Security System
keyAchievements.description: Legally established the Social Security System through the Social Security Act, promising economic security to the elderly, the poor, and the disabled.

keyAchievements.title: Improvement of labor standards
keyAchievements.description: Passed the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), outlawing child labor and establishing a federal minimum wage.

keyAchievements.title: Establishment of the Executive Office of the President
keyAchievements.description: Established the Executive Office of the President, the backbone of the federal executive branch, through the Reorganization Act of 1939.

keyAchievements.title: Environmental conservation efforts
keyAchievements.description: Encouraged the expansion and funding of national parks and national forests and made environmental conservation efforts through the Civilian Conservation Corps.

faq.question: Which president was Franklin D. Roosevelt?
faq.answer: He served as the 32nd President of the United States.

faq.question: What are the major achievements of Franklin D. Roosevelt?
faq.answer: He implemented the New Deal to overcome the Great Depression and led the Allies in World War II.

faq.question: How many times was Franklin D. Roosevelt elected president?
faq.answer: He was elected president a total of 4 times, the only person in US history to serve more than 3 terms.

faq.question: What disease did Franklin D. Roosevelt suffer from?
faq.answer: He was diagnosed with polio in 1921, which left his lower body paralyzed.

faq.question: Who was Franklin D. Roosevelt's wife?
When did Franklin D. Roosevelt die?
